stories.title: Judge says Trump must hand over financial records to BBC
stories.summary: MIAMI, Florida– A federal magistrate judge ruled Tuesday that President Donald Trump must turn over detailed financial information in connection with his defamation lawsuit against the BBC. U.S. Magistrate Judge Enjoliqué Lett concluded that Trump’s sweeping complaint put injury to his businesses at the forefront and made their vitality a fair subject for the BBC to demand information about. “All of President Trump’s brand, properties and businesses are impugned or

stories.title: ‘No electricity, no water, no fuel’: Ukrainian drone strikes bring darkness to Crimea’s summer
stories.summary: Interviews with residents of Russian-held region point to frustration with Moscow as Ukrainian campaign to isolate it takes toll on daily life First, the guests stopped coming. As Ukrainian drones and missiles pounded Crimea throughout the spring and into the summer, Russian tourists began cancelling their bookings at the small guesthouse Marina runs in Yevpatoria, a resort town on the peninsula’s Black Sea coast. Then petrol became scarce, making it harder to stock the business with food and ot

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: tankers carrying Saudi oil turned back after Houthi threats; Trump claims US will hit Iran nuclear storage facility ‘pretty soon’
stories.summary: In a letter to shippers, the Houthis have threatened to attack any ships that load or discharge Saudi oil Houthis threaten to attack shipping tankers if they use Saudi Arabian ports on Red Sea Lebanon’s president, Joseph Aoun, is due to meet Donald Trump for the first time today as he wraps up a four-day visit to Washington. The meeting coincides with ongoing US-mediated talks between Lebanon and Israel. The two sides announced a “framework agreement” on 26 June which stipulates the withdrawal o

stories.title: Taylor Swift kedvenc, megkínzott költője öngyilkos lett, életműve viszont máig sokakat inspirál
stories.summary: Költő és feminista ikon. Az Annie Halltól a Simpson családon át, a Szexoktatásig számos műben találkozhatunk a gondolataival, és számos szál köti Magyarországhoz is. Ismerjék meg Sylvia Plath-t, akiről nemrég Tamás Dorka kiadott egy tanulmánykötetet Nagy-Britanniában.

stories.title: Női beosztottjait környékezte meg a rendőrfőkapitány, akit már ketten is feljelentettek
stories.summary: Egy jelenleg is állományban lévő, és egy, az ügy miatt leszerelt rendőrnő is feljelentést tett az egyik megyei rendőrfőkapitány ellen, akinek július 15-én a belügyminiszter visszavonta kinevezését. A Telex információja szerint a Budapesti Regionális Nyomozó Ügyészség nyomozást indított az ügyben.

stories.title: A vérszívó, aki a színházi próbákon érezte igazán jól magát
stories.summary: Egy Nobel-díjas szerző keserű kísértetregénye, egy bestselleríró nagy visszatérése és a magyar származású hollywoodi legendáról szóló monográfia. Heti könyvajánló.

stories.title: A Fidesz a német és szovjet megszálláshoz hasonlítja az Alaptörvény-módosítást
stories.summary: A párt közleménye szerint az Alaptörvény-módosítás óta Magyarország már nem demokratikus jogállam, ellenállást hirdetnek.

stories.title: Havasi Bertalan: Magyar Péternek is több kását kell megennie, ha le akar számolni a nemzeti oldallal
stories.summary: A Fidesz szervereinek lefoglalásáról kérdeztük, kásaevési tanácsokat adott a „zsebdiktátornak” a Fidesz kommunikációs igazgatója.

stories.title: Dolgok, amiktől a magyar konyha sokkal különlegesebb volt a paprika térhódítása előtt: a tárkony
stories.summary: A rengeteg zöldfűszer iszonyú jót tesz csomó magyar ételnek a töltött káposztától a töltött paprikán át a főzelékekig és levesekig. Most azt is elmondjuk, hogyan lehet belőle ecet vagy mustár.

stories.title: Zelenszkij mégis leváltotta az ukrán hadsereg főparancsnokát
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök korábban inkább a védelmi minisztert menesztette, aki Olekszandr Szirszkij leváltását javasolta. A népszerű védelmi miniszter mellett tüntetések kezdődtek, Zelenszkij a jelek szerint részben engedve a nyomásnak megválik a hadsereg főparancsnokától.

stories.title: Hekkertámadás érte a román földhivatali rendszert, egy hete áll az ország ingatlanpiaca
stories.summary: Még mindig elérhetetlen a rendszer, a zsarolóvírusos támadó állítólag minden megszerzett adatot törölt. A felelős ügynökség szerdára ígért változást.

stories.title: A foci-vb végleg elszabadult
stories.summary: Eddig sem volt sok kétség, de a 2026-os világbajnokság mindennél világosabban megmutatta, hogy a FIFA-nak a pénz és a politikai érdek mindennél fontosabb. Pedig a balhék, botrányok mellett amúgy nem volt olyan rossz ez a vb.

stories.title: A Fidesz-kormány az EU legnagyobb államadósság-növekedését hozta össze az utolsó teljes negyedévére
stories.summary: Az idei első negyedévben egyetlen másik tagállamban sem nőtt akkorát a GDP-arányos államadósság, mint Magyarországon, államháztartási hiányban másodikok lettünk.

stories.title: Tanács Zoltán szerint az elmúlt napok sakkjátszmájában Polgár Judit és Magyar Péter is nyert
stories.summary: A miniszter szerint a társadalom viszont veszített.

stories.title: Az Operettszínház főigazgatója a társulat nyílt levelére: Nem gondolom, hogy egy ilyen nehéz helyzetben egymás ellen kellene harcolnunk
stories.summary: Kiss-B. Atilla szerint különbséget kell tenni Orbán János Dénes művészi teljesítménye és a más szervezettel kötött megkérdőjelezhető szerződései között.

stories.title: Adománygyűjtés indult a megrongált Máré-vár helyreállítására
stories.summary: A várat be kellett zárni, miután ismeretlen bulizók a várfalból bontott kövekből építettek tűzrakóhelyet.

stories.title: Behallatszott egy mikrofonba, ahogy Magyar Péter azt mondja: Itt a teljes sajtó, szigorúan két mondat
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a parlamenti technikai szünetben odalépett a tiszás képviselőkhöz, és megkérte őket, hogy csak röviden nyilatkozzanak a sajtónak.

stories.title: Hetekig rohadt a szemét a XXII. kerületi kukákban, mert túl sok kukás hiányzik a Mohu Budapesttől
stories.summary: Volt, akinél beköpték a legyek és nyüvek lepték el a ház előtt álló kuka tartalmát.

stories.title: Nincsen semmi bajom az alázattal. Jót tesz az egónak
stories.summary: Jeffrey Wright Oscar-jelölt színészt nem zavarják a mellékszerepek, sőt, néha jobban szereti, ha nincs a stáblista elején. Játszott az Éhezők viadalában, a Westworldben, Bond-filmekben, most pedig a második Batman-filmet forgatja. Interjú.

stories.title: Utolsó kísérletre készülhet Putyin Ukrajna ellen, de a milliós emberveszteséget az oroszok szeme elől sem lehet eldugni
stories.summary: Fordulóponthoz közelít a Kreml, Oroszországban a hadiállapot bevezetése sem kizárt, miközben Ukrajna a nehézségek ellenére fenntartja az ellenállást. Farkát csóváló labrador kölyökkutyaként Moszkvába igyekvő Szijjártóról, Magyarország orosz energiára építő eddigi függőségéről is beszélt az Oroszország-szakértő Rácz András a Telex Téma című műsorában.

stories.title: Az utolsó engedély is megjött, elkezdődhet a Városháza park megépítése
stories.summary: A park közel nyolcezer négyzetméternyi új zöldfelületet jelentene Budapest belvárosában.

stories.summary: Csendes korábban az MTÜ alá tartozó Visit Hungary vezérigazgatójaként dolgozott, idén februárban távozott a szervezettől.

stories.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
stories.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

stories.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
stories.summary: Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
stories.summary: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers

stories.title: Anas Sarwar reportedly set to quit as Scottish Labour leader to be minister in Andy Burnham’s government – as it happened
stories.summary: Sarwar expected to be elevated to Lords to take up a ministerial post at revamped Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade Burnham to cut VAT on electricity bills in first cost-of-living move António Costa, the president of the European council, said in a post on X that he held a “good” phone call with Andy Burnham last night, in which he said they agreed to “further strengthen” EU-UK relations and continue to cooperate to support Ukraine. He said there was also discussion about “c

stories.title: US immigration agents arrested a record number of people in June, data shows
stories.summary: Guardian analysis shows officers arrested 43,138 people as quiet and steady operations ramp up across the country The Trump administration arrested a record number of immigrants last month, according to newly released government data that reveals how US immigration agents are quietly but steadily ramping up operations across the country. A Guardian analysis of the latest data published by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) found that federal immigration agents arrested 43,138 people in

stories.title: Appeals court reverses decision that released pro-Palestinian Columbia activist Mohsen Mahdawi
stories.summary: The second circuit overturned the previous decision, saying the judge overstepped authority by intervening before it moved through the immigration court system A federal appeals panel has reversed a lower court decision ordering the release of pro-Palestinian activist Mohsen Mahdawi, opening the door to the re-arrest of a student protest organizer whom the Trump administration has spent more than a year trying to deport. Tuesday’s ruling from the second US circuit court of appeals in New York Ci

stories.title: Australia news live: Hanson says domestic violence is ‘wrong but it’s also a two-way street’ and links it to cost-of-living pressures
stories.summary: ‘Don’t put up with it,’ One Nation leader says. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns , says the state’s Labor party is under threat from One Nation and “we’ve got to fight for those votes”. Appearing on ABC Radio National this morning, Minns was asked if One Nation was a “racist party”. He said people who had concerns about the levels of immigration weren’t necessarily racist but some of the things Pauline

stories.title: Jordan Bardella to address Nigel Farage’s Reform UK conference
stories.summary: LONDON — Jordan Bardella will speak at Reform UK’s upcoming party conference in Birmingham, the British party confirmed Tuesday. Bardella, president of the far-right French party National Rally , will give an address in English to Nigel Farage’s supporters on Sept. 4, the second day of Reform’s three-day get-together. “I am pleased to welcome Jordan to our party conference,” Farage said in a statement. “He represents a new generation of political leaders who are standing up for

stories.title: Canada joins British-Italian-Japanese fighter jet project
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain, Japan and Italy are bringing Canada in on their project to field a sixth-generation fighter jet by 2035, defense ministers from the nations announced jointly on Tuesday. The move to include Ottawa as an observer nation — as first reported by POLITICO last week — is meant to strengthen middle-power supply chains. It marks the first move in the job by Britain’s new defense chief Wes Streeting, after Prime Minister Andy Burnham appointed him late Monday. “My focus is on maximizing

stories.title: Trump says midterms won’t impact his Iran war plan
stories.summary: President Donald Trump dismissed the idea that the upcoming midterm election could influence how he goes about fighting Iran, following over a week of escalating strikes and three U.S. soldiers being killed over the weekend. The president, taking questions in the Oval Office on Tuesday alongside Lebanese President Joseph Aoun, posited that Iran is “probably” trying to impact the results of the elections, before saying that “they won’t have any impact on me.” “No, the election — I

stories.title: Zelenskyy fires top army commander amid nationwide protests
stories.summary: KYIV — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Army Commander-in-Chief Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi on Tuesday, just a week after ousting popular Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Zelenskyy had cited the dysfunctional relationship between Syrskyi and the reformist minister as his reason for firing Fedorov on July 15. But the decision triggered a wave of protests demanding he be reinstated and Syrskyi removed instead. Zelenskyy has so far refused to return Fedorov to the defense ministry, saying

stories.title: Hegseth says Iran is weakened, but won’t give timeline for war’s end
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insisted on Tuesday that Iran was on the defensive, even as he declined to offer a timeline for when the war might end — and acknowledged that Tehran still poses a threat in the region. Iran’s military capabilities are “at the weakest point militarily” after nearly two weeks of new American airstrikes, Hegseth said. And he insisted that the White House is achieving its objective of preventing Iran from rebuilding its nuclear weapons programs and terrorizi

stories.title: The Queensland government promised to put victims first. But it has buried a report from domestic violence survivors
stories.summary: Exclusive: Leaked document from LNP-convened panel heavily criticises police responses to family violence and calls for civilian oversight Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Queensland government has buried the findings of its own working group of domestic violence victim-survivors after their report was heavily critical of police responses and called for a “civilian review mechanism” to escalate complaints abou

stories.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
stories.summary: Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Fiscal hawk or big spender – what kind of chancellor will John Healey be?
stories.summary: Diligent, popular, unexpected pick for the Treasury has been given a straitjacket – and the job of funding PM’s policy promises On the frequent occasions that John Healey would travel abroad as defence secretary, he was so determined to keep working he would get the crew to tether a desk to the inside of his military aircraft so he could read his papers. His diligence is one reason why Andy Burnham, the new prime minister, made Healey his unexpected pick for chancellor on Monday. Allies say othe

stories.title: Andy Burnham urges cabinet to examine ‘all possible ways’ to tackle cost of living crisis
stories.summary: As new prime minister briefs cabinet on ‘cost of living government’, critics ask where money will come from Andy Burnham is under mounting pressure to explain how he will pay for his policy pledges as he implored his new cabinet to examine “all possible ways” to tackle the cost of living crisis. On his first full day in power, the prime minister faced questions about how he would fund the removal of VAT from electricity bills and an expected uplift in defence spending – an issue that led his new

stories.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine’s army chief and offers Fedorov ‘prominent position’
stories.summary: After nationwide protests Oleksandr Syrskyi is to be replaced in reshuffle and popular defence minister returns to government Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests . In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces. Drapatyi would o

stories.title: India’s Cockroach protests: Rahul Gandhi detained after calling for PM Modi to resign
stories.summary: Main opposition leader is bundled into a bus by police during Delhi CJP protests after accusing Narendra Modi of ‘destroying the future of India’s youth’ Indian police have detained opposition leader Rahul Gandhi during spiralling youth-led protests in Delhi, after he called for prime minister Narendra Modi’s resignation. Gandhi, the leader of India’s Congress party, said on Tuesday that Modi must resign “for destroying the future of India’s youth”, before staging a sit-in outside the prime mini

stories.title: India: Police forcibly remove Rahul Gandhi from protest outside Modi residence
stories.summary: Rahul Gandhi and other leaders of the main opposition Congress party have been dragged away by police from a rally outside PM Narendra Modi's residence, a day after dozens of youth protesters were arrested.

stories.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
stories.summary: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s plans could bring the UK closer to Europe than he realises
stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: Protests in Italy after death of Moroccan man under police restraint
stories.summary: Teargas and water cannon used on protesters after Abderrahim ⁠Fakir died pleading for help from police Police have clashed with demonstrators during a protest in the Italian city of Bologna after a Moroccan-born man died pleading for help under police restraint. Human rights campaigners and Italy’s opposition parties have demanded urgent answers over the death on Sunday of Abderrahim ⁠Fakir, 42, who had lived in Italy since he was five years old and ran a small removals and cleaning business. Co

stories.title: Trump says he will impose tariffs on generic drugs starting in August 2028
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced a plan to impose a 100 percent tariff on generic drugs starting in August 2028 as part of a bid to push pharmaceutical production back to the United States. The tariff would then jump to 200 percent in 2029, if implemented, the president said in a social media post. “Effective August 1st, 2026, all Generic Drugs being brought into the United States will continue to have a TARIFF of ZERO PERCENT for a two year period of time, after which the T

stories.title: French environment minister to resign after passage of controversial farm bill
stories.summary: PARIS — French Minister of Ecological Transition Monique Barbut plans to step down in protest after lawmakers approved controversial farming legislation that exposed major divisions among President Emmanuel Macron’s centrist supporters. A member of the minister’s team, granted anonymity to discuss a decision that has not been shared publicly, said that Barbut told Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu and her own office she would resign after the bill’s expected passage by the French Senate later Tue

stories.title: Russian warship carries out rare live-fire exercise 45 miles off UK coast
stories.summary: New defence secretary Wes Streeting denounces drill by Neustrashimy as ‘performative and irresponsible’ A Russian warship fired its main cannon in a short notice test 45 miles south of Plymouth on Monday, the day Andy Burnham was appointed as prime minister. The Neustrashimy suddenly announced it intended to fire its guns in the morning and requested that a British warship, which had been tracking it, retreat a couple of miles to a safe distance. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer allies see ‘hint of retribution’ in Burnham reshuffle
stories.summary: Former PM’s supporters question pledge to end factional infighting after more than half his cabinet removed or demoted Allies of Keir Starmer have questioned Andy Burnham’s pledge to end factional infighting after he removed or demoted more than half of the outgoing prime minister’s cabinet in his first reshuffle . “This was a deeply factional cabinet reshuffle,” one said. “The idea it was anything else is ridiculous.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Former Met police chief admits surveillance of Stephen Lawrence’s family was institutionally racist
stories.summary: Paul Condon tells spycops inquiry force failed to properly control unit which committed ‘horrendous’ abuses A former Metropolitan police commissioner has admitted that the surveillance of the family of Stephen Lawrence by undercover officers was institutionally racist, the spycops inquiry has heard. While Paul Condon was in command of the Metropolitan police in the 1990s, a controversial undercover unit spied on the family as they sought to persuade Scotland Yard to properly investigate Lawrence

stories.title: Hegseth tells Senate war on Iran has so far cost $37.5bn at hearing to request more funds
stories.summary: Defense secretary has asked Senate appropriations committee for nearly $88bn more to continue effort The US defense secretary, Pete Hegseth told the Senate on Tuesday that the US-Israel war on Iran had cost $37.5bn so far, as he faced lawmakers furious over the conflict’s soaring price tag and the deaths last week of three American service members. During an appearance before the Senate appropriations committee, Hegseth, whose opening remarks were repeatedly interrupted by protesters, provided a

stories.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
stories.summary: The ribbon-cutting celebration of the Gordie Howe International Bridge is no longer taking place after Trump announced a 50% tariff on Canadian goods A planned joint US-Canada ceremony Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. stories.title: Mamdani says New York City does not have legal authority to arrest Netanyahu
stories.summary: Mayor had said he was reviewing ability to execute arrest warrant issued by the ICC for Israeli prime minister Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year. “Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to social media. stories.title: Trump announces new tariffs on generic drugs to take effect in 2028 – US politics live
stories.summary: President says 100% tariff would be imposed on generic drugs made overseas after 1 August 2028 ‘for a one year period of time, and 200% thereafter’ Sign up for US Breaking News email US gas prices have continued to rise since the conflict with Iran resumed this month. According to the American Automobile Association (AAA), a gallon of fuel is now just over $4 –  almost a dollar more than a year ago. It comes as Brent Crude , the international oil benchmark, is now up by almost 2% to $90.92

stories.title: US claims of Cuba’s ‘unique threat’ jar with reality of hunger and blackouts
stories.summary: State department’s 100-page report is latest push in Trump administration campaign to bring down government The US government’s latest allegations that Cuba is behind “many of the most significant upheavals in recent American political history” and that its leaders pose “a unique threat to American homeland security” have come as startling revelations to those living under the six-month-long US oil blockade in Havana, where desperate people search through piles of uncollected rubbish for food, o

stories.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag re-arrested in Turkey
stories.summary: The latest arrest comes two months after he was released from detention on charges including "insulting" President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

stories.title: Germany records rise in sexual violence against children
stories.summary: Germany's Criminal Police Office has reported over 17,000 cases of sexual violence against children in the past year. Organizations such as Zartbitter provide support to victims.

stories.summary: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

stories.title: Italy: Protests in Bologna over death of immigrant in police custody
stories.summary: Demonstrators are demanding answers over the death of 42-year-old Abderrahim Fakir. Local authorities have urged calm while the investigation is underway.

stories.summary: Experts warn global energy market at a ‘critical juncture’ with escalating Middle East crisis expected to further drag on a slowing Australian economy Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The likelihood of a Reserve Bank interest rate hike has doubled, according to market forecasts, as the escalating Middle East crisis once again drives fuel prices higher amid dwindling global oil reserves. stories.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
stories.summary: Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open A Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington. The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator. Continue reading.

stories.summary: A woman ostentatiously making her own pasta in an eaterie in Florence or Rome might seem the height of authenticity. But travellers, beware ... Name: Pasta windows. Age: Pretty new. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Warning to shipping companies, on top of Iran’s chokehold on strait of Hormuz, may severely affect Saudi oil exports Middle East crisis live – latest updates Yemen’s Iranian-aligned Houthis have warned shipping companies that their tankers risk being attacked if they use Saudi Arabian ports, as the militia moved quickly to enforce a blockade announced the previous day. The Houthi warning for the Red Sea, coming on top of Iran’s threat to shipping on the other side of the Arabian peninsular in th

stories.title: Airbus to test folding wings for next generation of bestselling planes
stories.summary: Such technology solves problem of longer, thinner, energy-saving wings not fitting in airports around the world Airbus has said it will test folding wings for the next generation of its bestselling planes, as the world’s largest planemaker races to work out the shape of future commercial aircraft by the end of the decade. Longer, thinner wings made of light but strong composite materials offer the promise of energy savings but present a problem for manufacturers because they will not fit in airp

stories.title: France becomes first EU country to ban social media access for under-15s
stories.summary: Parliament votes to stop children using apps such as TikTok in policy championed by Emmanuel Macron France’s parliament has approved a bill banning social media access for children under 15, making it the first EU country to bar children from apps such as TikTok. stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC

stories.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
stories.summary: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

stories.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
stories.summary: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.

stories.title: This farmer wanted to quit the cocaine industry - he couldn't
stories.summary: Colombians who grow cocaine's raw material bemoan a lack of support to help them switch crops.

stories.title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
stories.summary: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.

stories.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
stories.summary: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Conti

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Trump, not Iran, is the world’s greatest danger. He’s a one-man weapon of mass destruction | Simon Tisdall
stories.summary: As the bombing starts again, it’s clear the president has dragged the US into a limitless fiasco – and the world into an economic quagmire Feckless and clueless, Donald Trump is lost in Iran, unable to find a way out of the disastrous war he started. Once again, the US military is pummelling the country and, increasingly, its civilian infrastructure. As before, this unlawful bludgeoning strengthens the resistance of a hardline regime that cares little for its people’s suffering. How often have T

stories.title: Dismissal of Ukraine’s defence minister highlights wider issues for Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov, celebrated by many for innovative, tech-driven approach, was sidelined for military old guard Volodomyr Zelenskyy’s abrupt dismissal of Ukraine’s youthful and innovative defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, at precisely the moment Kyiv appeared to be gaining advantages in several spheres of its war with Russia has exposed, not for the first time, a troubling flaw in the president’s leadership. The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and d

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
stories.summary: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca J

stories.title: Woman speaks out on husband being pulled from Ryanair plane – video
stories.summary: Svetlana Grković and her husband, Ljubisa Karović, had just settled into a Ryanair flight last week when a loud bang pierced the hum of engines. Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked out of the plane through a dislodged cabin window. Karovic is now being treated in hospital in Thessaloniki with severe neck and arm injuries, Maksimovic said ‘If we die, we die together’: wife of Ryanair passenger almost sucked through window speaks Continue reading...

stories.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
stories.summary: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

stories.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

stories.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
stories.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

stories.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
stories.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

stories.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
stories.summary: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

stories.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

stories.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
stories.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

stories.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
stories.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

stories.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
stories.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

stories.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
stories.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

stories.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
stories.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

stories.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
stories.summary: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

stories.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
stories.summary: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

stories.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
stories.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

stories.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix
